source_metadata.title_raw: Douglas B. Lyon - I-19-090 - August 22, 2019 - Claremont

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
August 22, 2019 
Douglas B. Lyon 
Planning Commissioner 
612 Wellesley Dr. 
Claremont, CA 91 711 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-19-090 
Dear Mr. Lyon: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 Because your question is general in nature, we are treating 
your inquiry as a request for informal assistance. 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (197 5) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
· not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
May you participate in Planning Commission deliberations and decisions related to the City 
of Claremont's existing Old School House Specific Plan where your residential property is located 
within 500 feet of the specific plan boundary? 
CONCLUSION 
No. Where your residence is located within 500 feet of the specific plan area, and the project 
plans to revitalize the area (including replacing a vacant hotel with new housing, adding a Trader 
Joe's grocery store, and renovating existing commercial complexes), the evidence that the project 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 ofthe California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. I-19-090 
Page No. 2 
will have minimal impacts in some respects does not refute that the revitalization of the specific 
plan area will have a measurable impact on your financial interest in your residence. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
Background: 
The City's Old School House/Claremont Inn Specific Plan ("OSHSP") involves a 21-acre 
site and was originally approved in 2006. The Specific Plan calls for a "revitalization" of the area 
and includes renovations to the Doubletree Hotel (formerly the Claremont Inn), renovation of the 
. 
. 
. 
OSHSP retail and office complex, and construction of a new retail building that includes Trader 
Joe's Market. It also involves the conversion of a vacant three-story hotel and surface parking lot 
into 30 condominium units and a two-level parking garage. This construction will be adjacent to 
Colby Circle. Street construction and improvements, and the demolition of 17,200 square feet of 
existing structures are also planned. 2 
On July 11, 2017, the City Council approved a tentative subdivision application which 
includes the condominium and parking structure construction and the creation of a new private road 
connecting southwest Colby Circle to Indian Hill Boulevard. 3 The City's Resolution noted the 
following: The new private road will be in addition to Colby Circle (which will remain in place as a 
public street). It also calls for developing the DoubleTree Hotel and Old School House "into a 
unified complex, with open space, landscape, and water features that will make it unique in the 
region and attract visitors and the community." The condominium housing will integrate affordable 
housing with market rate housing units. This will provide affordable housing within an area of the 
City currently lacking affordable housing. In approving the application, the City noted that the 
public streets have sufficient capacity to absorb traffic generated by this project. 
Residential Property 
You serve as a City planning commissioner. Your residence is located 408 feet to the north 
of the OSHP boundary. Your residence is buffered from the OSHSP area by existing two-story 
homes, condominiums, apartments, and mature shade trees. Currently you have no information 
about particular decisions relating to the OSHSP which may come before the Planning 
Commission. 
You state that your parcel is entirely independent of the OSHSP. No changes are 
contemplated that would involve improved services to your residence. All the planned changes will 
be internal to the OSHSP area and most of the changes that will take place further than 500 feet 
from your residence. You believe that "the distance is such that impacts to the viewscape and 
privacy would be nonexistent and that impacts due to noise levels, air quality, or traffic would be 
2See City's Old School House Development website page: https://www.ci.claremont.ea.us/living/development-
projects/old-school-house-development, as viewed May 8, 2019 
3 See City's Resolution No. 2017-45 at hltps://w\ w.ci.claremont.ca.us/home/showdocument?id=944 I, as 
viewed May 8, 2019. 


File No. I-19-090 
Page No. 3 
negligible." Further, you do not believe that either the character of your property or its market value 
would be affected by activities, or changes in activities, within the OSHSP area. 
After discussing this matter by phone, you provided additional information from the City's 
Old School House/Claremont Inn Revitalization Specific Plan, the California Environmental 
Quality Act (CEQA) Initial Study Checklist and the Mitigation Negative Declaration to support 
your understanding of the project's anticipated impacts. For example, the Mitigation Negative 
Declaration shows that most aspects of the OSHSP are anticipated to have no impacts, or less than 
significant impacts, with a few items listed as "less than significant with mitigation" regarding 
items such as noise, housing populations, and transportation/traffic issues. This is largely due to the 
fact that the OSHSP area has been in use as a hotel/commercial area and the construction of the 
condominium units and parking will have similar impacts. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on one or more of 
the public official's interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) You have identified that you 
have a real property interest in your residence. (Section 87103(b).) You have not identified a 
particular decision at issue. 
Foreseeability 
, 
Based on the facts you have provided, your residence will not be explicitly involved as a 
named party in, or subject of, decisions relating to OSHSP. (Regulation 18701(a).)4 For a financial 
interest that is not explicitly involved in the decision, as anticipated here, the financial effect of the 
decision on an official's interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701 (b ). ) It is a realistic 
possibility that OSHSP decisions will have a financial impact on your real property interest. 
Materiality: Real Property Interest 
Regulation 18702.2(a) provides a list of circumstances under which the reasonably . 
foreseeable financial effect of a governmental decision on an official's real property interest is 
material. Regulation 18702.2( a)(7) states that the financial effect is material if the decision: 
(7) Involves property located 500 feet or less from the property line of the parcel 
unless there is clear and convincing evidence that the decision will not have any 
measurable impact on the official's property; · 
4 A financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, 
denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest, or if the 
decision affects a real property interest described in Regulation 18702.2(a)(l)-(6). (Regulation 18701(a).) 


File No. I-19-090 
Page No. 4 
Here, the OSHSP is within 500 feet of your residence and the decisions will generally apply 
to the OSHSP area. You have provided evidence that the impacts of the OSHSP will be "less than 
significant," on items such as noise, housing populations, and transportation/traffic issues given the 
past commercial use of the area. However, we must assess the potential effects beyond these items. 
Based upon the facts provided, the project is of considerable scale and in close proximity to your 
residence. The City is seeking to revitalize an area with a vacant hotel, construct new condominium 
housing including affordable housing, add commercial retail such as a Trader Joe's Market and 
renovate existing commercial buildings. The City anticipates that the OSHSP project will transform 
the area so that it will attract visitors and the community. As such, the evidence provided does not 
overcome the presumption that a decision will have a measurable impact on your residential 
property under Regulation 18702.2(a)(7). Therefore, you have a reasonably foreseeable and 
material financial interest in the OSHSP decisions. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
LKH:aja 
Sincerely 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
/ £_~ 
l~;;--
L. Karen Harrison. 
Counsel, Legal Division

plan area will have a measurable impact on your financial interest in your residence.

of Claremont's existing Old School House Specific Plan where your residential property is located 
within 500 feet of the specific plan boundary?

CONCLUSION: No. Where your residence is located within 500 feet of the specific plan area, and the project 
plans to revitalize the area (including replacing a vacant hotel with new housing, adding a Trader 
Joe's grocery store, and renovating existing commercial complexes), the evidence that the projecwill have minimal impacts in some respects does not refute that the revitalization of the specific 
plan area will have a measurable impact on your financial interest in your residence.
